description_long: This is a multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each separated by prominent, upward-curving eaves. The roof sections are deep blue with gold trim, and the building sits on an octagonal gray base. Each level features black railings and window-like openings, giving the impression of balconies or walkways around the perimeter. The topmost section is crowned with a golden ornamental finial, adding a decorative flourish to the apex. The overall design is symmetrical and highly stylized, reflecting traditional East Asian architectural motifs. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from stone or concrete, with wooden or metal accents and a tiled roof.
description: A five-story blue-roofed pagoda with gold trim, black railings, and an ornate golden finial, set on an octagonal base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical five-tiered blue roof, gold finial, and octagonal base.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the depth of the eaves and the layered structure of the pagoda.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the stacked stories and the continuity of the black railings.
description_view_3: View similar to the front, highlighting the upward-curving eaves and the central finial.
